purpose: Portable context for AI systems that cannot browse the live GitHub Pages app.
review_prompt: You are reviewing a static, local-first universal profile intake and review system. Do not assume you can browse the live URL. Use this packet as the source of truth. Evaluate whether the language is clear for nontechnical users and whether the schema remains powerful enough for advanced canon, asset, graph, AI, game, and export workflows. Flag corporate/HR/personality-test/fantasy-only language, privacy risks, confusing required fields, missing consent guardrails, and any gaps for reviewing real people, fictional characters, NPCs, historical figures, AI agents, family members, partners, children, collaborators, and pets.

app_summary.title: Universal Profile Intake + Review System
app_summary.intake_page_visible_text: HTF / K Street Character Database HTF / K Street Character Database This shared profile system turns real group lore into consistent characters, dialogue, art prompts, NPCs, relationship maps, and project memory across HTF, K Street, West Coast Crypto Cruise, and future weird ambitious builds. Person schema 3.0.0 with Knowledge Graph v4 foundation. MODE Simple Intake Advanced Canon Agent Export Save Locally Load Locally Review Submissions Sections Simple Member Intake Fill in what feels easy. The point is to capture what makes you recognizable in the group lore: your vibe, voice, obsessions, road-trip energy, Discord behavior, questionable superpowers, and how you show up when a plan gets weird. What will this be used for? Approved answers may help create consistent characters, dialogue, art prompts, NPC behavior, project memory, and relationship maps for shared HTF / K Street projects. Consent fields decide what can be stored, fictionalized, simulated, or reused. Easy path: add your display name, three-word vibe, current obsession, legendary story, visual description, voice style, group/project context, and consent choices. Then click Submit Descriptor . If you want to share a reference photo, describe where the reviewer can find it or send it privately. Public repos should not contain real private images unless explicitly approved. Advanced Canon Editing Full person schema, asset/reference metadata, canon controls, source notes, timeline entries, and graph-aware entity linking for HTF / K Street maintainers and collaborators. Agent Export Console Generate clean JSON, Markdown, visual references, graph files, and one bundled context block for ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, local agents, game tools, and project imports. Submit Descriptor Generate Character Summary Copy Shareable Profile Generate AI Context Generate Art Prompt Generate Dialogue Prompt Generate NPC Summary Generate Relationship Summary Generate Visual Reference Sheet Generate Character Context Generate Organization Summary Generate Project Summary Generate Timeline Summary Generate Lore Summary Generate AI Agent Context Import JSON Reset Asset Library Manager Drag files here or choose files to add asset metadata Choose Asset Files Asset search Filter by type All types Filter by canon All canon levels Filter by consent All consent statuses Filter by project Filter by tag Filter by creator Filter by date Gallery View Table View Bulk Tag All Filtered Bulk Set Consent/Canon Bulk Delete Filtered Select an asset to preview metadata. Knowledge Graph Graph search Entity type filter All entity types Network View Timeline View Entity Explorer Select a node or relationship to inspect it. Agent Export / Import Profile JSON Profile JSON Compact JSON Copy JSON Markdown And Agent Context Markdown Profile AI Context Copy Markdown Copy All Agent Context Game, Art, Dialogue Game Design Summary Art Prompt Dialogue Prompt NPC Summary Relationship Summary Visual Reference Sheet Art Reference Pack Sprite Reference Assets And Graph Relationships JSON Assets JSON Asset Manifest Graph JSON Graph People Graph Relationships Graph Organizations Graph Locations Graph Events Graph Assets Graph Projects Validation, Sharing, Import Download Everything Validate JSON Copy Share Link Import JSON Search fields Schema version Migration report GitHub Pages base URL Latest share link Submission status Live JSON Preview Completeness: 0% Ready.
app_summary.review_page_visible_text: Profile Review Dashboard Profile Review Dashboard Private review workflow for moving profiles from Raw to Reviewed to Approved for Use. Static, local-first, JSON/Markdown portable. Open Intake Form Import JSON Files Save Review Queue Locally Load Local Queue Clear Queue Profiles Import downloaded Formspree/profile JSON files. Consent first, privacy second, canon/project approval third. Drag JSON profiles here All statuses Raw Reviewed Approved Review Checklist Confirm consent before any real person becomes reusable canon. Keep optional contact, real names, boundaries, and private source image notes private. Confirm which projects can use the profile and how visible the adaptation can be. Add reviewer notes, confidence, canon level, and final status. Export Raw, Reviewed, or Approved packages into the private project archive. Ready. Selected Profile Select a profile from the left. Nothing here writes to GitHub or a server.

schema_summary.title: HTF / K Street Person Profile v3
schema_summary.description: Canonical source-of-truth profile schema for HTF / K Street people and shared characters across narrative, game, AI, relationship, and memory systems.

simple_intake_sections.title: Consent
simple_intake_sections.note: The yes/no stuff that keeps the group from accidentally turning private lore into public canon.

fields.label: Okay to fictionalize me
fields.help: Required before adapting into stories, games, or NPCs.

fields.label: Okay to use my vibe for AI dialogue
Okay to use visual notes for art prompts
fields.help: Controls whether visual prompt fields can be reused.

fields.label: Okay to reuse across group projects
fields.help: Useful for shared HTF / K Street profiles.

fields.label: Okay to map relationships and dynamics
fields.help: Allows graph and social model simulation.

fields.label: Consent notes / weird edge cases
fields.help: Records usage limits, approvals, and review context.
fields.placeholder: Okay for fictionalized group lore; keep real names, family details, and private relationship stuff out of public exports.

simple_intake_sections.title: Identity
simple_intake_sections.note: The public-safe handle, vibe, obsessions, and table-intro version of the person.

fields.label: What should we call you?
fields.help: Public-safe display name, handle, or nickname.
fields.placeholder: Maya / @packetghost / whatever people actually call me

fields.label: Best way to ask follow-up questions
fields.help: Private follow-up contact if the person permits clarification.
fields.placeholder: Discord handle, Signal, email, or 'ask me in the group chat'

fields.label: Character handle / codename
fields.help: Reusable character handle for fiction, games, and prompts.
fields.placeholder: Terminal Wizard, Roadtrip Bard, Meme Archaeologist

fields.label: Two-sentence lore intro
fields.help: A friendly one-paragraph intro in the person's own words.
fields.placeholder: I fix weird problems, ask too many questions, and somehow become useful during chaos. I am usually holding coffee and three unfinished ideas.

fields.label: Three words that describe you
fields.help: Fast vibe check for people, writers, and NPC systems.
fields.placeholder: Chaotic, creative, caffeinated

fields.label: Animal you identify with
fields.help: Totem, mascot, self-own, or recurring group bit.
fields.placeholder: Crow, raccoon, wolf, octopus, coyote, house cat, honey badger, pigeon of destiny

fields.label: Current obsession
fields.help: The topic currently eating your browser tabs.
fields.placeholder: Local AI models, parkour history, retro RPG design, Bitcoin self-custody, urban exploration, weird internet archaeology

fields.label: Pronouns or how people should refer to you
fields.help: Supports respectful dialogue and profile generation.
fields.placeholder: they/them, he/him, she/her, name only, no preference

fields.label: Current base / usual orbit
fields.help: Current public-safe location or usual region.
fields.placeholder: San Diego, Austin, the road, airport lounges, wherever the group chat sends me

simple_intake_sections.title: Appearance
simple_intake_sections.note: Visual anchors for art, prose, sprites, and memory. Think recognizability, not biometric paperwork.

fields.label: Default outfit
fields.placeholder: black hoodie, conference badge, trail shoes, cargo pants, jacket with too many pockets

fields.label: Signature item if we only draw one prop
fields.help: One prop that says 'yes, that is them.'
fields.placeholder: Coffee thermos, camera, notebook, hardware wallet, multi-tool, suspiciously heavy backpack

fields.label: Default expression
fields.help: A fast visual anchor for illustration and NPC idle states.
fields.placeholder: half amused, half concerned, already debugging the situation

fields.label: Movement / body language
fields.help: How the character occupies space and communicates nonverbally.
fields.placeholder: paces while thinking, perches on furniture wrong, talks with hands, moves like the floor is optional

fields.label: Visual description for artists / AI
fields.placeholder: modern internet nomad, parkour-adjacent posture, travel-worn clothes, bright eyes, late-night build energy

simple_intake_sections.title: Personality
simple_intake_sections.note: Vibe, useful chaos, recurring patterns, and the things the group already knows are true.

fields.label: Vibe tags
fields.help: Useful trait tags for filtering and generation.
fields.placeholder:
curious
stubborn
reliable
chaotic
creative
caffeinated
quiet
analytical
dangerous

fields.label: Oddly specific traits
fields.help: Nuanced traits that standard lists miss.
fields.placeholder:
can smell bad incentives
answers Discord at impossible hours
keeps receipts in the spiritual sense

fields.label: What do people always come to you for?
fields.help: Competencies that shape story and mechanics.
fields.placeholder:
fixing servers at 2 AM
finding cheap flights
talking people off ledges
remembering obscure lore
turning chaos into a plan

fields.label: Failure modes / recurring bugs
fields.placeholder:
overcommits to the bit
forgets sleep is load-bearing
buys the top
turns every problem into a research spiral

fields.label: Character archetype
fields.help: The slightly mythic group-chat version of this person.
fields.placeholder: Accelerator Ghost, Terminal Wizard, Logistics Goblin, Chaos Engineer, ZK Monk, Meme Archaeologist, Burnout Prophet, Startup Necromancer, Desert Mystic, Roadtrip Bard, Vibe Cartographer, Parkour Historian

fields.label: Signature ability
fields.help: The thing they can do that feels unfair but somehow real.
fields.placeholder: Can find WiFi anywhere. Knows somebody who knows somebody. Solves impossible problems at 3 AM. Generates ideas faster than consequences.

simple_intake_sections.title: Social Model
simple_intake_sections.note: How the profile bonds, disagrees, cares, and fits into groups.

fields.label: Friendship style
fields.help: How they show up for friends.

fields.label: How you connect to the circle / projects / scenes
fields.help: Plain-language relationship context for intake, review, and graph migration.
fields.placeholder: HTF / K Street friend, Discord regular, WCCC contributor, parkour-adjacent chaos witness, conference hallway ally.

fields.label: How people know you care
fields.help: Care language for dialogue and relationship simulation.
fields.placeholder: Makes the plan easier, remembers who needed a ride, checks on basics, sends the useful link at exactly the right time.

simple_intake_sections.title: Humor And Voice
simple_intake_sections.note: How they sound in the group chat, on a road trip, during a build sprint, and after the plan has obviously gone sideways.

fields.label: Voice rhythm
fields.placeholder: starts with 'okay so' then builds a whole theory; pauses for the bit; gets very precise when stakes rise

fields.label: Typical phrases
fields.placeholder:
This seems like a bad idea. Let's do it.
I have concerns, but not enough concerns.
We're already committed.
Nobody learns from success.

fields.label: Topics you can talk about for hours
fields.placeholder:
Parkour
AI agents
Crypto
Philosophy
Street photography
UFO lore
Fitness
History
Startups
Open source software
Dreams
Human behavior

fields.label: Representative quotes
fields.placeholder:
How hard could it possibly be?
That went better than expected.
Let's never do that again.
See? Completely under control.

fields.label: Battle intro quote
fields.help: A heightened game-style line that still sounds like them.
fields.placeholder:
This seems like a bad idea. Let's do it.
I have concerns, but not enough concerns.
We're already committed.

fields.label: Victory quote
fields.help: What they say after surviving the situation.
fields.placeholder:
Nobody learns from success.
That went better than expected.
Let's never do that again.

fields.label: Dialogue should do this
fields.placeholder:
sound like a real friend
include practical weirdness
know the lore
keep the humor human

fields.label: Dialogue should avoid this
fields.placeholder:
do not make them a tech bro caricature
do not turn every line into a pitch
do not make private lore public

fields.label: Baseline emotional weather

all_form_sections.title: Motivation
all_form_sections.note: What the person optimizes for, protects, refuses, and is trying to become.

fields.label: What actually matters to you?
fields.help: Ordered by priority. Include built-ins or custom values.
fields.placeholder:
friendship
freedom
curiosity
building real things
not leaving people stranded
truth even when annoying

fields.label: What keeps pulling you forward?
fields.placeholder: Make confusing situations survivable for the people they love.

fields.label: What they optimize for
fields.help: What they instinctively try to protect or improve.
fields.placeholder: Good signal, good people, enough battery, enough runway, nobody stranded at the wrong airport.

fields.label: What they refuse to compromise
fields.placeholder: They will not betray a vulnerable ally for convenience.

fields.label: What tempts them
fields.placeholder: Being needed, being right, having the answer first.

fields.label: What they are trying to become
fields.placeholder: Someone who trusts others enough to share the load.

fields.label: Greatest need
fields.help: What they emotionally need even if they do not say it.
fields.placeholder: Permission to not be useful every second.

fields.label: Greatest fear
fields.placeholder: Being useless when people are counting on them.

fields.label: Moral center
fields.placeholder: Do not abandon people just because the plan got inconvenient.

fields.label: Shadow desire
fields.help: The desire that could become dangerous if unchecked.
fields.placeholder: To control the room so no one can fail.

all_form_sections.title: Decision-Making
all_form_sections.note: How the profile chooses, fails, recovers, and handles risk.

fields.label: How you choose a path
fields.help: How they usually choose a path.

fields.label: Risk appetite
fields.help: Risk appetite for story and mechanics.

fields.label: How far ahead are you thinking?
fields.help: How far ahead they naturally think.

fields.label: What happens when the plan catches fire?
fields.placeholder: Gets quieter, prioritizes essentials, delegates cleanly, checks who has water and who has the keys.

fields.label: Common blind spot
fields.placeholder: Assumes everyone wants a solved problem more than emotional processing.

fields.label: Failure mode
fields.help: How they break down or make mistakes.
fields.placeholder: Takes over and becomes terse.

fields.label: Recovery pattern
fields.placeholder: Apologizes with a concrete repair plan and revised boundaries.

fields.label: What makes them act
fields.help: The trigger that moves them from thought to action.
fields.placeholder: A friend is at risk and no one owns the next step.

fields.label: What makes them freeze
fields.help: The kind of uncertainty or fear that stalls them.
fields.placeholder: Competing needs where every option hurts someone.

fields.label: Trust breakers

performative loyalty
needless secrecy

fields.label: Boundary style

fields.label: Loyalty triggers

protecting someone vulnerable

fields.label: Betrayal triggers
fields.placeholder:
needless secrecy
using someone as a tool

all_form_sections.title: Conflict And Stress
all_form_sections.note: How pressure changes behavior and what restores trust.

fields.label: When challenged, you usually...
fields.placeholder:
research until the room gets tired
debate the premise
take control of logistics
make a joke to lower the voltage

fields.label: How friends can tell you are stressed
fields.placeholder:
shorter sentences
less eye contact
more lists
starts cleaning the kitchen
opens the same tab six times

fields.label: What makes it worse
fields.placeholder:
bad-faith arguments
avoidable risk to friends
people pretending there is no problem
last-minute mystery plans

fields.label: What helps you come back down
fields.placeholder:
clear facts
food
a concrete next step
someone taking one task off the board
five minutes outside

fields.label: What you are good at when things get tense
fields.help: What they do well in conflict.
fields.placeholder:
separates facts from panic
names the real decision
protects the quiet person in the room

fields.label: Conflict weaknesses
fields.placeholder:
can become terse
may over-control

fields.label: How you repair after a bad moment
fields.placeholder: Direct apology, changed process, and a follow-up message after everyone has slept.

fields.label: How your voice changes under stress
fields.help: How their voice changes under stress.
fields.placeholder: Shorter, more procedural, fewer jokes, more 'okay, first we solve the immediate problem.'

all_form_sections.title: Narrative Engine
all_form_sections.note: The lore layer: stories people retell, chaos patterns, running bits, and what changes over time.

fields.label: What are you usually chasing?
fields.help: What they would pursue in story terms.
fields.placeholder: Build something that probably should not work, make it useful, then accidentally turn it into a community.

fields.label: Growth arc
fields.placeholder: Learns to stop treating burnout as proof of commitment.

fields.label: Legendary story people tell about you
fields.help: A story that already sounds like a side quest.
fields.placeholder: Accidentally slept through a conference and still made the keynote. Drove across three states for a single taco recommendation. Started a business from a Discord joke.

fields.label: Most chaotic moment
fields.help: A memorable moment where the plan became lore.
fields.placeholder: Missed a flight and accidentally joined a startup. Lost a wallet, found a friend. Ended up at Burning Man with 24 hours notice.

fields.label: Recurring internal conflict
fields.placeholder: Wants peace but keeps volunteering for impossible projects because the group needs someone with a spreadsheet and a car.

fields.label: Shadow version
fields.placeholder: Becomes a sleep-deprived chaos engine who mistakes urgency for meaning.

fields.label: Best-self version
fields.placeholder: A grounded builder who can hold ambition, friendship, and rest at the same time.

fields.label: Running joke
fields.placeholder: Always says they are leaving in ten minutes, then becomes load-bearing to the next three-hour conversation.

fields.label: Symbols / motifs
fields.placeholder:
gas station coffee
conference badge
open tabs
rooftop at sunrise
dusty road
half-built prototype

fields.label: Themes
fields.placeholder:
friendship under stress
ambition without permission
community as survival tech
making meaning from chaos

fields.label: Dramatic question
fields.placeholder: Can you build impossible things without becoming impossible to live with?

fields.label: What actually changes them?
fields.help: Forces that actually move the character.
fields.placeholder: A friend calling them out with love, a project failing honestly, a road trip that creates enough silence to think.

fields.label: What never changes?
fields.placeholder: They will always notice the person quietly falling out of the group orbit.

fields.label: Role in the group story
fields.placeholder: The one who knows the lore, finds the workaround, and somehow has the charger.

fields.label: Scene where you shine
fields.placeholder: 3 AM kitchen table after the conference, everyone tired, the plan broken, and suddenly they make it make sense.

fields.label: Scene that breaks your brain
fields.placeholder: A high-stakes group decision with bad WiFi, no clear owner, and three people pretending they are fine.

all_form_sections.title: Generated Content
all_form_sections.note: Editable generated outputs. Buttons at the top can fill these from the structured fields.

entity.notes: Fictional sample root person entity with HTF / K Street / WCCC flavor.

relationships.notes: Sample graph link for a friend-circle profile.

consent.consent_notes: Sample record only. Do not treat as a real submission. Keep private contact, real names, source images, and boundaries out of public exports unless explicitly approved.

identity.short_bio: A sleep-deprived internet friend who fixes weird problems, finds the useful room at conferences, and turns Discord jokes into actual project plans.
identity.current_obsession: Local AI models, Bitcoin self-custody, retro RPG design, parkour history, and weird internet archaeology.
identity.current_location: Usually between the group chat, a hacker house kitchen, and the crypto conference hallway track
identity.origin_location: Parkour forums and long road trips

appearance.height_build: medium height, parkour-adjacent posture, hard to lose in a crowd
appearance.hair: messy travel hair, usually under a beanie by day three
appearance.eyes: alert, sleep-deprived, always scanning for outlets, exits, and the bit
appearance.face_features: expressive eyebrows, too-aware resting face, looks like they just read the room and the room failed QA
appearance.distinguishing_marks: old conference wristband, scuffed shoes, hoodie with mystery pockets
appearance.silhouette: hoodie, backpack, forward lean, looks ready to climb a wall or fix a router
appearance.everyday_clothing: black hoodie, conference badge, trail shoes, cargo pants, jacket with too many pockets
appearance.iconic_outfit: sun-faded camp shirt, beat-up sneakers, sunglasses at night, backpack that has seen things
appearance.accessories: camera, pocket notebook, hardware wallet, carabiner, old lanyard
appearance.carried_items: half-charged laptop, coffee thermos, phone with 200 open tabs, multi-tool
appearance.default_expression: half amused, half concerned, already debugging the situation
appearance.movement_body_language: paces while thinking, perches on furniture wrong, talks with hands, moves like the floor is optional
appearance.image_prompt_notes: modern internet nomad, hacker-house roadtrip energy, conference badge, travel-worn hoodie, bright tired eyes
appearance.negative_image_prompt_notes: avoid corporate mascot, resume headshot, generic tech bro, quest hero cosplay, or villain caricature

narrative_engine.shadow_version: sleep-deprived chaos engine who mistakes urgency for meaning
narrative_engine.best_self_version: grounded builder who can hold ambition, friendship, and rest at the same time
narrative_engine.running_joke: always says they are leaving in ten minutes, then becomes load-bearing to the next three-hour conversation
narrative_engine.dramatic_question: can you build impossible things without becoming impossible to live with?
narrative_engine.what_changes_them: a friend calling them out with love, a project failing honestly, a road trip that creates enough silence to think
narrative_engine.what_never_changes: they will always notice the person quietly falling out of the group orbit
narrative_engine.role_in_group_story: the one who knows the lore, finds the workaround, and somehow has the charger
narrative_engine.ideal_scene: 3 AM kitchen table after the conference, everyone tired, the plan broken, and suddenly they make it make sense
narrative_engine.nightmare_scene: a high-stakes group decision with bad WiFi, no clear owner, and three people pretending they are fine

active_abilities: turns a joke into a prototype
traversal_abilities: navigates cities by vibes and coffee shops
social_abilities: knows when the bit has gone too far
special_dialogue_triggers: someone says 'how hard could it be?'
failure_comedy_mechanics: gets on the wrong bus but finds better lore
forgets the charger but has three adapters
game_mechanics.power_curve: starts as a useful weirdo, becomes the person everyone quietly routes impossible problems through
game_mechanics.balance_notes: strong improvisation should cost sleep, battery, social credit, or snacks

ai_generation.canonical_short_description: A sleep-deprived builder with road-trip instincts, Discord lore, and the suspicious ability to make bad plans useful.
ai_generation.canonical_long_description: A capable internet friend shaped by hacker houses, parkour culture, startup chaos, crypto conferences, AI projects, and late-night conversations.
ai_generation.art_prompt_summary: modern hacker-house roadtrip character, conference badge, travel-worn hoodie, bright tired eyes, backpack full of impossible tools
ai_generation.dialogue_prompt_summary: write like a real group-chat regular: specific, funny, tired, capable, slightly too online, never generic
ai_generation.behavioral_prompt_summary: protect the group, follow the weird lead, ask practical questions, keep the humor sharp, and remember friendship is part logistics
ai_generation.game_design_summary: support/chaos utility character who converts bad plans into survivable adventures and unlocks hidden social routes
do_not_misrepresent: Do not make them a generic tech bro
Do not reveal private contact information

project_usage.project_relevance_notes: Good sample for cruise NPC energy, group memory, social cameos, late-night dialogue, and support-character writing.
project_usage.project_specific_notes: Okay as a background NPC on West Coast Crypto Cruise; ask before making this sample a main character or romance subplot.

source_notes.source_notes: fictional sample with HTF / K Street / WCCC voice; no real person or private image included

generated_content.character_summary: Packet Ghost is a sleep-deprived fixer whose road-trip instincts, weird internet memory, and deadpan timing turn group chaos into usable lore.
